name: Lean Prove (decompose → retrieve‖synthesize → verify → compare → abstract → bank)
description: The full Phase-5 prover loop. The decomposer writes a have-chain scaffold of typed sub-goals; skeleton_check type-checks it in skeleton mode; then for the sub-goal, retrieve (Path A) and synthesize (Path B) run in ONE wave (they share skeleton_check as their only upstream, so _wave_groups runs them concurrently = Path A ‖ Path B for free); verify lists BOTH as upstream and acts as the native controller — the kernel is the verdict, the handler owns Path-A-next-best vs Path-B-repair routing and the bounded repair loop (delegating only the proposal to the repair agent), and (in RESEARCH mode) verifies both paths so compare has a genuine contest; compare picks the more-general/shorter winner deterministically and logs the (retrieved, synthesized, winner) thesis triple; abstract is a native controller that — whenever Path B produced a fresh verified lemma (anti-starvation, even when Path A won) — lifts its constants to variables via the abstractor agent, re-verifies, and keeps the most-general form that type-checks (falling back on over-abstraction); bank assembles the sorry-free result.lean from the concrete winner and stores the abstracted (generalized) lemma. Multi-sorry theorems fan out as one (retrieve‖synthesize→verify→compare→abstract) chain per sub-goal over the shared, sub-goal-namespaced blackboard.

nodes.instruction: Read plan.md from the workspace. Take the first active sub-goal's lean_type as your goal. Write a complete, self-contained Lean 4 proof of exactly that proposition with NO sorry. CRITICAL: use ONLY core Lean 4 (Nat, and tactics like rfl, simp, decide, omega, induction) and do NOT add ANY `import` statements — Mathlib is NOT available to the verifier, so any import line makes verification fail. The 'source' field must contain just the theorem declaration with no import lines and no leading blank lines. Save it to the shared task context under the key 'candidate_b' as a JSON object {"source", "statement", "proof_term", "origin": "synthesize"} via the context_put tool.
